top_headline: FROM HORMUZ TO BARAKAH — UAE NUCLEAR PLANT STRUCK; NVDA T-1; FOMC MIN T-1
chapters: I · MACRO (pages 02-03) — Warsh Week 2 opens: ES -0.41% / NQ -0.30% futures; TNX 4.60% (1-year high territory, +0.01pp); DXY ~99.35; SPX May 18 close 7,403.05 (-0.07%); Dec 2026 rate-hike probability now ~40% (CME FedWatch), up from 30-35% last week; June hold ~97%; FOMC minutes Wednesday 2pm ET (Apr 28-29 8-4 dissent autopsy); Gold $4,542 May 18; Warsh QT-for-Cuts framework intact, no Day 1 speech, first FOMC June 17

II · AI TRADE (page 04) — NVDA T-1 to May 20 AH earnings; closed ~$222 on May 18 (-3.4% from PM high $229.72); Q1 FY2027 consensus $78.5B rev (+79% YoY) / $1.77 EPS (+127% YoY); Goldman raised EPS to $1.86; Citi Q1 upside $80.2B; Q2 FY27 guide consensus $86.08B is the swing number; H200 China: 10 firm licenses approved, Beijing purchase pause active — May 20 call is only China guidance forum; implied move ±12.9% (~$28/share) — Market Chameleon; NVDA historically declines more often than rises post-earnings even on beats

III · EARNINGS (pages 05-06) — Today: HD Q1 FY2026 BMO ($41.6B / $3.41 EPS consensus, -4% YoY EPS, housing headwinds, TNX 4.60% 30Y mortgage, options ~5% move); KEYS Q2 FY2026 AH ($1.71B / $2.32 EPS consensus, AI T&M demand); Tomorrow: NVDA AH (see Chapter II); BIDU Q1 reported May 18: EPS $11.90 (est $11.80 beat); Rev RMB 31.62B (est 31.97B inline miss); AI-powered business +49% YoY to record RMB 13B+; stock +4% PM; CSCO +14% AH May 14 (AI infra blowout); AMAT +8% AH May 14 record $7.91B industry growth >30%

IV · GEOPOLITICS (pages 07-08) — Barakah nuclear plant drone attack May 17: three drones, two intercepted, one fire in external generator at UAE's Al Dhafra facility (Arab world's only nuclear plant, 25% UAE electricity); IAEA confirmed radiation normal; reactors operational; Brent spiked to $111.50 intraday May 18; Iran MOU 14-point memo stalled: Tehran hardliners blocking; US military briefing Trump on operations; 14-point MOU framework: Iran enrichment moratorium + uranium to Russia; US lifts sanctions + frozen funds; both lift Hormuz restrictions; deal target $85-90 Brent (ING); no-deal target $115-120; IEA undersupplied through October; 4M bbl/day Hormuz flow loss

tase_events.event: Barakah nuclear plant drone attack May 17 = direct Israeli security premium escalation; Iranian drone operations in Gulf = elevated Elbit Systems/Rafael/Israeli defense sector risk premium; Brent ~$109-111 sustained = Israeli energy import cost pressure; TNX 4.60% = global rate-risk headwind for TA-35 growth names; TA-35 tracking global risk-off tone

narrative_threads: Geopolitical escalation ladder: Hormuz closure (structural) → Barakah nuclear plant drone attack → US military Sledgehammer contingency → Iranian hardliner resistance to MOU; each rung up = higher Brent floor, more Warsh rate-hike pressure; deal closure = largest single macro relief event of the cycle

NVDA May 20 binary: T-1 setup shows stock drifting back from PM highs ($229.72 → $222); market not pre-pricing a blowout; the China guidance wildcard (H200 Beijing purchase pause) is the differentiator between beat vs. transformational beat; Q2 guide $86B+ is the only number that prevents a sell-the-news reaction

Warsh inflation inheritance — Week 2: CPI 3.8% + PPI 6.0% + Brent $111 + Barakah attack = Warsh cannot credibly point to inflation resolution at any near-term FOMC; hike probability now 40% is structurally driven, not tail-risk; FOMC minutes tomorrow will codify the hawkish language direction

AI capex validation wave: BIDU AI +49%, CSCO +14%, AMAT +8% record — three independent confirmations of AI demand floor before NVDA; sets extremely high bar but also structural floor under demand narrative; if NVDA beats AND guides up, three-leg stool (demand, supply, China) confirmed
